questions.question: What does `AS RESTRICTIVE` mean in a PostgreSQL RLS policy?
options: The policy only applies to SELECT queries
The policy must be satisfied even when other permissive policies allow access
The policy applies to all roles including superusers
questions.answer: The policy must be satisfied even when other permissive policies allow access
questions.explanation: RESTRICTIVE policies form an AND condition with all other policies. No permissive policy can override a restrictive policy. This guarantees workspace isolation cannot be bypassed.

questions.question: What is the third parameter of `set_config('app.current_workspace_id', id, true)` for?
options: Enables encryption of the setting value
Makes the setting visible to all database users
Scopes the setting to the current transaction only
Sets the setting globally for all sessions
questions.answer: Scopes the setting to the current transaction only
questions.explanation: The third parameter `true` means `is_local = true`. The setting is transaction-scoped and automatically reset when the transaction ends. This prevents workspace context from leaking between requests in a connection pool.

questions.question: What pgvector index type does Meridian use for similarity search?
questions.explanation: HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) provides approximate nearest neighbor search with better query performance than IVFFlat. Created with: CREATE INDEX ON user_memory USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops);

questions.question: What `ON DELETE` behavior must the `audit_log` table use for `user_id` and `workspace_id` FKs?
questions.explanation: ON DELETE SET NULL preserves audit records when users or workspaces are deleted. Cascade would destroy the audit trail, which violates compliance requirements.

questions.question: Explain why the application DB user must have `rolbypassrls = false`. What would happen if it were true?
questions.answer_key: If rolbypassrls = true, all RLS policies are silently ignored for that role. Every query would return all rows regardless of workspace_id, completely defeating tenant isolation. The app user must have rolbypassrls = false so that RLS policies are enforced on every query.
questions.rubric: Full credit: explains what BYPASSRLS does AND the tenant isolation consequence. Partial: states it bypasses RLS without explaining the consequence.

questions.question: What are the three memory tiers in Meridian, and what is different about each tier's lifecycle?
questions.answer_key: Session: cleared explicitly at end of conversation. Medium: evicted after 30 days of inactivity (last_accessed_at), LRU eviction. Deep: permanent, requires explicit promotion signal, never auto-evicted.
questions.rubric: 1 point per tier with correct lifecycle description. 3 points total.
